findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting/suite_runner.py has no test file — critical path for pytest streaming and retry logic is untested
evidence: Grep for 'suite_runner' in tests/infra_tests returns zero matches
suite_runner.py implements run_pytest_stream (binary non-blocking select loop) and run_test_suite (retry/coverage-conflict logic) — both are high-importer code paths
infrastructure/reporting/suite_runner.py is imported by scripts/01_run_tests.py (the primary test runner)
findings.suggestion: Add tests/infra_tests/reporting/test_suite_runner.py covering: run_test_suite with a trivially-passing pytest invocation in tmp_path, the coverage-conflict retry branch, and the check_test_failures halt path. Use real subprocess calls per the no-mocks policy.

findings.summary: test_environment.py docstring says 'Python 3.8+' but pyproject.toml requires >=3.10 — stale assertion documentation
evidence: test_environment.py:25: comment '# We're running on Python 3.8+, so this should pass'
findings.suggestion: Update the inline comment to '# Python 3.10+ required per pyproject.toml' so it stays consistent with the declared constraint.

findings.summary: The logging subsystem is split into five peer files (logging_utils.py, logging_formatters.py, logging_progress.py, logging_helpers.py, logging_constants.py) inside infrastructure/core/ — a flat overload with 117 total importers pointing at logging_utils as the hub
evidence: Glob shows all five logging_*.py files live flat in infrastructure/core/ alongside 20+ unrelated modules
logging_utils.py has 135 importers (god module per holistic_context); it re-imports from the other four logging_* siblings
grep 'from infrastructure.core.logging_' shows 129 occurrences across 117 files — all routing through this flat namespace
The five files have clear internal cohesion (constants -> helpers -> formatters/progress -> utils) but no subdirectory boundary to make that hierarchy navigable
findings.suggestion: Move the five logging_*.py files into infrastructure/core/logging/ with an __init__.py that re-exports the public surface (get_logger, setup_logger, format_duration, etc.). This isolates the logging subsystem boundary without changing any external import paths that use logging_utils.

findings.summary: StreamingProgress.__init__ retains a multi-line Args docstring on a straightforward constructor that only assigns fields — verbosity not justified by complexity
evidence: logging_progress.py:228-237: StreamingProgress.__init__ has a 9-line docstring with Args section listing total/message/stream/update_interval for a constructor whose entire body is 5 field assignments
By contrast, Spinner.__init__ (same file, line 123) has no docstring at all — inconsistent verbosity within the same module
findings.suggestion: Replace the multi-line Args docstring on StreamingProgress.__init__ with a single-line summary or no docstring, matching the Spinner.__init__ convention in the same file.
